etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 包(包) (bāo) -> foam
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") relates to the character's meaning of foam or bubbles, which form in water. The phonetic component 包(包) (bāo) provides the sound. Together, they effectively convey the concept of bubbles or foam forming in water.

examples.chinese: 杯子里有很多泡(杯子裡有很多泡)。
examples.pinyin: Bēizi lǐ yǒu hěn duō pào.
examples.english: There are many bubbles in the cup.

examples.chinese: 这个肥皂能产生丰富的泡沫(這個肥皂能產生豐富的泡沫)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège féizào néng chǎnshēng fēngfù de pàomò.
examples.english: This soap can produce rich foam.

examples.chinese: 用热水泡脚可以缓解疲劳(用熱水泡腳可以緩解疲勞)。
examples.pinyin: Yòng rèshuǐ pào jiǎo kěyǐ huǎnjiě píláo.
examples.english: Soaking your feet in hot water can relieve fatigue.
